title: Casal Marskan
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image= |name=Casal Marskan |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], [[Horuz system/Legends|Horuz system]]''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' establishes that the events of [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]{{'s}} third tour of duty take place between the [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|capture of Leia Organa]] and the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Both of these events occur in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', and ''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' mentions Casal Marskan was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in the [[skirmish in the Horuz system]] from that part of the game. |species= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' |pronouns=He/him{{CSWECite|II|291|Casal Marskan}} |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings=[[Ernek Marskan]] |children= |affiliation=*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] **[[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|The Force is the ultimate power. With it, you can destroy your enemies. Just open yourself to it, let it guide you, and you'll be unbeatable.''"
"''Is that your secret, sir? I mean, it's well known that you've never lost a ship or failed a mission.''"
"''Yes. The Force is the secret. Let it guide you. Listen for the voice inside that tells you about the power. It's all there. You don't really have to do anything but pay attention.|Keyan Farlander and Casal Marskan|}} '''Casal Marskan''' was a [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilot]] in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] who served aboard the [[MC80A Home One Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Casal was the younger brother of [[Ernek Marskan]], another [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] serving aboard the ''Independence''. [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]] pilot [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]], having begun to experiment with the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], promoted aggression in Marskan and several other rookie pilots. He was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], along with fellow rookie [[Horodi]], while assisting Farlander in a [[Skirmish in the Horuz system|mission]] to capture a [[Ethar|pair]] of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|corvettes]], and Ernek blamed Farlander for his brother's death. ==Biography== ===Alliance service=== Casal Marskan was the younger [[Gender/Legends|brother]] of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Ernek Marskan]], a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] in [[Blue Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Blue Squadron]] aboard the [[MC80A Home One Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']]. Like Ernek, he opposed the ruling [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] prior to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], following Ernek into the [[Alliance Starfighter Corps/Legends|Alliance Starfighter Corps]] and service aboard the ''Independence''. [[File:KeyanFarlander.jpg|200px|thumb|Keyan Farlander, who selected Casal Marskan for a mission in the Horuz system]] Shortly after the [[Battle of Kalla VII|capture]] of the Imperial [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|frigate]] [[Priam (starship)|''Priam'']] around [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], Marskan was one of several new [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilots]] who joined Captain [[Samuel Raider]] and the [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] [[Speedy]] in the pilots' lounge aboard the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] vessel. [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron's]] Captain [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]] had recently discovered his ability to manipulate [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and had fallen under the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side's]] influence, causing Raider to become suspicious of the pilot. When Farlander entered the pilots' lounge, Raider stood up to leave, prompting a brief altercation between the two, during which Farlander attempted to use the Force to [[Mind trick/Legends|make]] Raider stay. Though Marskan and the others witnessed the confrontation, they were unaware of the nature of the two pilots' disagreement and joined Farlander in laughing off the incident. However, Farlander was hoping to create an invincible [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of pilots guided by the Force and began to encourage Marskan and the other pilots to learn to use the Force against their enemies, telling them that the Force was the reason he had never failed a mission or lost a starfighter. Though Marskan was initially skeptical about what Farlander had to say, he soon began to listen to the older pilot's advice. The ''Priam''{{'s}} capture gave the Alliance a lead on locating the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], an Imperial [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] capable of destroying a [[Planet/Legends|planet]], and two [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvettes]] from ''[[Ethar]]'' group were dispatched to the [[Prison/Legends|prison]] planet [[Despayre]] in the [[Horuz system/Legends|Horuz system]] to investigate. However, the [[Corvette/Legends|corvettes]] were captured by [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial forces]]. A few [[Standard day/Legends|days]] after he began to encourage aggression in the rookie pilots, Farlander was asked to lead a team three-ship group of Red Squadron's[[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']] [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] and guard an [[Skirmish in the Horuz system|operation]] to recapture the corvettes. Farlander selected Marskan, along with another rookie named [[Horodi]], to accompany him on the mission, which was also in 0 [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|BBY]]. ===Skirmish in the Horuz system and death=== [[File:Farlander2.jpg|thumb|left|250px|An X-wing is destroyed during Marskan's final mission.]] In order to clear out any defenses, the [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] proceeded into the area ahead of the rest of the [[Alliance Military/Legends|Rebel forces]], and they emerged from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] to find two Imperial [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttles]] in the process of boarding the corvettes. Several [[Alpha-class Xg-1 Star Wing/Legends|Assault Gunboats]] that were guarding the Imperial operation moved quickly to intercept the new arrivals. The X-wings [[Starfighter combat/Legends|engaged]] the Gunboats but were outnumbered by the Imperials, and both Marskan and Horodi were [[Death/Legends|killed]] during the dogfight. Farlander was also forced to eject from his X-wing, though the Rebels were successful in recapturing the corvettes and were able to retrieve the extra-vehicular pilot. The elder Marskan blamed Farlander for his brother's death, confronting him upon his return to the ''Independence''. However, Farlander, still influenced by the dark side, considered their deaths proof that both pilots were unworthy of the Force. Although Farlander eventually rejected the dark side, his attitude to the deaths of his comrades caused many of the other pilots to join Marskan in displaying animosity toward him. ==Personality and traits== Like many other rookies aboard the ''Independence'', Casal Marskan admired the more experienced pilots, including Farlander. Initially being the only rookie pilot to voice doubts over Farlander's theories concerning the Force, Marskan soon began to take Farlander's advice, which his brother later believed was the cause of Casal's death. ==Equipment== Marskan flew a T-65 X-wing starfighter. ==Behind the scenes== Casal Marskan was created as an unseen X-wing pilot for the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']], which was released in [[February]] [[1993]].{{LucasArts|url=20th/history_2.htm|text=20th Anniversary History, Part Two: The Classics, 1990 - 1994}} In [[June]] of the same year,{{CargoBay|url=item-detail/46|text=X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide}} the character was identified in the strategy book ''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'', written by [[Rusel DeMaria]]. The book also established Marskan as one of the aforementioned X-wing pilots. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']] {{1st}} {{C|[[Starfighter/Legends|Starfighter]] only}} ==Sources== *''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' {{1stID|Casal Marskan}} *''[[X-Wing Collector's CD-ROM: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Marskan, Casal}} [[Category:Individuals of unspecified species]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Red Squadron personnel]]
